specific_location: Jumbo Reservoir near bathroom (Logan Cty side)
description:
Bird was secretive most of the time. Only once did it perch
on some vegetation (see photos). Most of the time it was on the ground hiding
under a Russian Olive or looking for seeds in the exposed sand/soil out of
sight behind the tree. It was associating with American Tree Sparrows and
Dark-eyed Juncos.A smallish sparrow with a compact shape, large flat crowned
head and short tail. Dull pinkish seed bill with buffy/brown auriculars, lores
and smudgy streaking on dirty white chest. Obvious white eye ring. The back is
mottled tan, brown and back without obvious streaks. The wind has two distinct
buffy wing bars with a dirty white, chin, flank and belly. The crown was dark
with a light median and lateral crown stripes.It was windy, 10-20 MPH, so if it vocalized, I didn’t hear
it.
